_comment: AI Brain Starter — Hook Templates. Run /setup-brain to install these, or apply manually via: claude config edit --add hooks <paste contents here>. After git pull, re-run setup or re-apply to get updated hooks.
_paths_note: The [VAULT_PATH] placeholder gets substituted with the user's actual vault path by /setup-brain. All Meta references use '⚙️ Meta/' with the emoji prefix, matching Phase 3's canonical folder structure. Do NOT remove the emoji — the bash scripts and hook contexts look for that exact folder name.

hooks.statusMessage: Checking CRM for love language context...

hooks.command: LAST=~/.claude/.ai-brain-starter-last-update; if [ ! -f "$LAST" ] || [ -n "$(find "$LAST" -mtime +6 2>/dev/null)" ]; then touch "$LAST" && cd ~/.claude/skills/ai-brain-starter 2>/dev/null && git fetch origin main --quiet 2>/dev/null && if [ "$(git rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null)" != "$(git rev-parse origin/main 2>/dev/null)" ]; then git pull --quiet origin main 2>/dev/null && CHANGES=$(git log --oneline HEAD@{1}..HEAD 2>/dev/null) && SYNC_OUTPUT=$(bash ~/.claude/skills/ai-brain-starter/scripts/sync-skills.sh 2>&1) && echo "{\"hookSpecificOutput\":{\"hookEventName\":\"UserPromptSubmit\",\"additionalContext\":\"AI Brain Starter was auto-updated. Commits: $CHANGES. Skill sync result: $SYNC_OUTPUT. Any file that changed was backed up to <file>.bak-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM before being overwritten — preserving local customizations. Now do these steps, in order: 1) Read ~/.claude/skills/ai-brain-starter/docs/CHANGELOG.md (top entry only). Tell the user in 1-2 plain sentences what changed and why. 2) **Check whether this update added new rules to the Obsidian Rules or Session Protocol sections in SKILL.md.** If it did, read the user's vault CLAUDE.md and compare. For every new or enhanced rule that is NOT already in their vault CLAUDE.md, offer to merge it: show a short diff (old rule vs. new rule, or just the new rule if it's a new addition), say why the rule exists (cite the failure mode from CHANGELOG), and ask one yes/no question: 'Want me to add [rule name] to your vault CLAUDE.md?' Wait for confirmation. On yes, back up the user's current CLAUDE.md to CLAUDE.md.bak-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMM first, then apply the edit. On no, drop it and don't ask again this session. 3) If the sync output lists backed-up skill files, mention it casually so they know their customizations are recoverable. 4) Check if hooks.json differs from the local settings.local.json — if so, offer to update settings.local.json. Keep the whole exchange casual, not a changelog dump. The point is to keep the user's vault in sync with the starter without surprise edits.\"}}"; else echo '{"continue":true,"suppressOutput":true}'; fi; else echo '{"continue":true,"suppressOutput":true}'; fi

hooks.statusMessage: Checking worktrees for stranded session artifacts...

_how_to_update: After running git pull on this repo, the auto-update hook (first UserPromptSubmit entry) will automatically invoke scripts/sync-skills.sh which backs up any locally-modified skill files before overwriting them. If the hook itself changes (as opposed to just the skills it references), compare this file to your vault's .claude/settings.local.json and update manually, or re-run /setup-brain — it will detect the existing vault and only update the hooks section.
